overview:
The implementation of the C&D Claim Evaluator follows a defense-in-depth architecture, prioritizing strict data compartmentalization, legal accuracy, and user transparency. The system consists of a frontend web interface hosted on business.gov, an API gateway with prompt injection filters, an orchestration layer for document parsing, and an isolated LLM environment.

Sequencing is divided into four phases:
1. **Governance & Legal Prep**: Securing legal advice, mapping legislation, and establishing accountability.
2. **Secure Build**: Procuring the model, implementing strict data handling (balancing privacy destruction with Commonwealth recordkeeping), and building the UI with persistent disclaimers.
3. **Testing & Pilot**: RAI acceptance testing, fault injection, and a restricted pilot deployment.
4. **Production & Monitoring**: Continuous validation, distinct AI logging, and active kill-switch readiness.

steps.title: Establish governance, legal mapping, and accountability
steps.detail: Before development begins, designate an accountable official and use case owner, and establish a governance board to review the system every 12 months. Report the High-risk use case to the DTA. Conduct a comprehensive legal review to map obligations under the Privacy Act 1988, anti-discrimination laws, and Australian Consumer Law. Obtain formal legal advice to review the AI's programmed legal interpretations, draft robust disclaimers, and ensure compliance. Concurrently, consult human rights and anti-discrimination advisors alongside the Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA).

traces_to.mitigation: Designate accountable official/owner, report to DTA, and establish 12-month review cycle via governance board
Document relevant legislation including Privacy Act, anti-discrimination laws, and Australian Consumer Law
Obtain legal advice to review AI interpretations, draft disclaimers, and ensure liability compliance
Consult human rights and anti-discrimination advisors alongside the PIA

steps.title: Procure model and verify secondary use prohibitions
steps.detail: Execute an AI Suitability Assessment to evaluate data residency and privacy trade-offs. Inspect any pre-trained or external models in a secure development zone using approved scanners to detect malicious code prior to deployment. Verify contractually and technically that the AI system's terms of use strictly prohibit the developer or third parties from accessing input text for model training or secondary purposes.

traces_to.mitigation: Conduct AI Suitability Assessment evaluating data residency and privacy
Inspect pre-trained models in a secure dev zone using approved scanners
Verify terms of use strictly prohibit input text being used for model training or secondary purposes

steps.title: Develop transparent UI and non-AI alternatives
steps.detail: Build the web-based chat interface on business.gov with a persistent, highly visible disclaimer stating the tool is AI-generated, informational, and not a substitute for professional legal counsel. Configure the AI output to highlight specific paragraphs in the uploaded letter that lack legal basis and provide direct hyperlinks to relevant business.gov guidance articles. Ensure a static, comprehensive 'self-help' guide is published and easily accessible as a non-AI alternative.

traces_to.mitigation: Implement clear, persistent disclaimer that output is informational and not legal counsel
Inform users they are interacting with AI and provide a static self-help guide as a non-AI alternative
Highlight specific paragraphs in the upload and link to business.gov guidance articles for explanation

steps.title: Implement secure data handling and compliant recordkeeping
steps.detail:
To satisfy both privacy destruction requirements and Commonwealth recordkeeping obligations, implement a dual-path data lifecycle. 

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant WebApp as business.gov UI
    participant AI as AI Processing Enclave
    participant CRMS as Commonwealth Records System
    User->>WebApp: Uploads C&D Letter
    WebApp->>AI: Process Document (Encrypted)
    AI-->>WebApp: Returns Analysis
    WebApp->>CRMS: Archive Session (Prompts, Inputs, Outputs, Metadata)
    CRMS-->>WebApp: Ack
    WebApp->>AI: Trigger Hard Delete
    WebApp->>WebApp: Purge Local Cache
```

Ensure all data is encrypted at rest and in transit. Immediately upon session termination, transfer the AI-generated outputs, metadata, prompts, and inputs to an approved, access-controlled records management system, then securely delete all traces from the active web and AI processing environments.

traces_to.mitigation: Encrypt data, implement secure access controls, and destroy/de-identify personal info immediately after session ends
Capture AI outputs, metadata, prompts, and inputs into an approved records management system

steps.title: Deploy security controls, prompt defenses, and kill-switch
steps.detail: Implement input validation and system prompt guardrails to mitigate direct and indirect prompt injection vulnerabilities from uploaded documents. Build a manual kill-switch accessible to the business.gov operations team to immediately take the chatbot offline if it generates harmful advice. Integrate this kill-switch and automated rollback procedures into existing incident response and disaster recovery plans.

traces_to.mitigation: Address direct and indirect prompt injection vulnerabilities
Implement manual kill-switch, automated rollbacks, and integrate into incident response plans

steps.title: Execute RAI acceptance testing and data quality validation
steps.detail: Prior to deployment, execute a rigorous testing regime. Conduct Responsible AI (RAI) Acceptance Testing to verify the accuracy of the legal analysis. Perform negative testing, failure testing, and fault injection to identify document parsing vulnerabilities. Validate data quality by ensuring the system accurately parses PDFs, covers diverse legal scenarios and language styles to prevent bias, and maintains traceability of which data source influenced specific outputs.

traces_to.mitigation: Conduct RAI Acceptance Testing, negative testing, failure testing, and fault injection
Ensure data accuracy, completeness, bias coverage, and traceability

steps.title: Train operators and execute restricted pilot
steps.detail: Develop and deliver formal training for business.gov operators and the legal team on how to supervise the system, monitor outputs, and trigger the kill-switch. Following training, launch the system in a structured pilot phase to a restricted audience. Use this pilot to refine model accuracy, test the effectiveness of the UI disclaimers, and validate data handling controls before full public release.

traces_to.mitigation: Establish formal training for operators and legal team to supervise and intervene
Conduct a structured pilot phase before full public deployment

steps.title: Implement continuous monitoring and distinct AI logging
steps.detail: Deploy a Continuous RAI Validator pattern to monitor the chatbot in production. Configure logging to track AI decisions for compliance and forensic analysis, ensuring the AI's identity is recorded distinctly from user identifiers. Continuously monitor hallucination rates and the accuracy of legal guidance, maintaining disaster recovery and backup processes.

traces_to.mitigation: Establish Continuous RAI Validator, disaster recovery, and monitoring processes
Configure logging to track AI decisions with a distinct AI identity
